zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh Fraser 4th of Lovat於1341年出生在英國蘇格蘭音巴內-爾時，他的父親Alexander Fraser 3rd of Lovat18歲，母親de Moray18歲。他於1371年與Isabell Wemyss of Kincaldrum, latterly Lovat在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1410年3月30日在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾波奧利享年69歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭音巴內-爾。

nameMeanings.name: Fraser
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: apparently a nickname from Anglo-Norman French fraser(e), fresere ‘strawberry plant’. Fresel and Frisel, from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, are early variants of the Fraser surname, the modern Gaelicized form of which is Friseal. See Frizzell . The crest on the Scottish family's coat of arms takes the form of a strawberry plant, but its antiquity is unknown. Claims of a habitational derivation, in particular from a place called la Frézelière in Anjou (France), are attractive but they lack verifiable evidence.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

History: There are two clans Fraser in Scotland, with common ancestry going back to Simon Fraser of Keith in East Lothian, who lived in the 12th century. One of these clans has its seat at Philorth Castle (subsequently re-named Cairnbulg) on the northeast coast of Scotland. Sir Alexander Fraser, 8th laird of Philorth (c. 1536–1623) converted the fishing village of Faithlie into the burgh of Fraserburgh in the 1590s. The other clan is Fraser of Lovat, associated mainly with the city of Inverness are. They are descended from Simon Fraser, a younger son, who lived in the early 14th century. In Gaelic, the head of Clan Fraser of Lovat is known as Mac Shimi ‘son of Simon’.

variants.name: Frazer
meanings: Variant of   Fraser  , associated chiefly with northern Ireland.

variants.name: Frashier
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frizzell
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Old French fresel, frisel ‘lace, ribbon, braid, brooch, frill, ruff’, for someone with an ornate style of dress, or ‘object of little value’ (a diminutive of frese, of ancient Germanic origin).
Scottish: from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, an alternative form of Fraser .

variants.name: Frazier
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasher
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .
Possibly an altered form of German Fratzscher (see Frutchey ).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh Fraser 4th of Lovat was born in 1341, in Inverness-shire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Alexander Fraser 3rd of Lovat, was 18 and his mother, de Moray, was 18. He married Isabell Wemyss of Kincaldrum, latterly Lovat in 1371, in Inverness-shire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 30 March 1410, in Beauly, Inverness-shire, Scotland, at the age of 69, and was buried in Inverness-shire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh Fraser 4th of Lovat est né en 1341, à Inverness-shire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alexander Fraser 3rd of Lovat, avait 18 ans et sa mère, de Moray, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Isabell Wemyss of Kincaldrum, latterly Lovat en 1371, à Inverness-shire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 30 mars 1410, à Beauly, Inverness-shire, Écosse, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Inverness-shire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

März 1410 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Beauly, Inverness-shire, Scotland und wurde in Inverness-shire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.Iona, Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 이오나
ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Fraser 4th of Lovat(이)가 1341년 영국 스코틀랜드 인버네스-실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander Fraser 3rd of Lovat(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 de Moray(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1371년 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르에서 Isabell Wemyss of Kincaldrum, latterly Lovat(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1410년 3월 30일 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르 보리에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 인버네스-실르에 묻혔다.

ru.Iona, Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom: иона, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh Fraser 4th of Lovat родился в 1341 г. в местности инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Alexander Fraser 3rd of Lovat было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали de Moray, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabell Wemyss of Kincaldrum, latterly Lovat в 1371 г. в местности инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 30 марта 1410 г. в местности болы, инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
